data.content_ref: inline:The claim asserts that tin supply from Central Asian networks terminated abruptly after 1180 BCE, causing a bronze shortage that crippled elite armament production. The forbidden case only guards against isotopic evidence of continued Anatolian tin sourcing post-1150 BCE. The account is fatally exposed to a different refutation: if destruction layers at the collapsed palace centres contain substantial hoards of bronze weaponry and intact bronze objects abandoned in situ, this demonstrates that bronze scarcity was not the proximate cause of collapse—elites possessed bronze but could not or did not use it. The target's single forbidden case fails to exclude this direct archaeological falsification, leaving the mechanism empirically unguarded.
